title: Developer Graph — Technology Foundation for Social Collaboration Fabric
context: ADR 016 defined the sandbox Cell model. The next layer is the Developer Graph — the pseudo-social layer where people and Cells discover each other, establish trust, form work contexts, and route collaboration. This is not a social network. It is a social collaboration fabric for executable projects. The network can route attention and proposals, but it cannot mutate local truth. Deep research (June 2026) validated all major technology choices against the 2026 landscape: AT Protocol DIDs proven at 43M+ Bluesky user scale, W3C DID v1.1 at Candidate Rec, UCAN spec stable at v1.0.0-rc.1, Yjs dominant at 920K weekly downloads with Yrs Rust rewrite, and event sourcing with PGlite as established PostgreSQL event store pattern.
decision: ### Technology Stack\nSee docs/adr/017-developer-graph.md for full decision text. Deep research (June 2026) validates all technology choices against the 2026 landscape: AT Protocol DIDs at 43M+ Bluesky scale, DID v1.1 W3C Candidate Rec, UCAN v1.0.0-rc.1 spec stable, Yjs 920K weekly downloads, event sourcing with PGlite as established pattern.
consequences: See docs/adr/017-developer-graph.md for full consequences. Positive: portable identity, auditable event-sourced graph, UCAN scoped authority, no external protocol dependency, dominant CRDT ecosystem. Negative: AT Protocol still standardizing, UCAN less mature than OAuth, projection maintenance overhead, GitHub OAuth bootstrap retains centralized trust root.
